author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I am bit confused on using `should_extend_payload` to decide what the `bid.parent_block_hash` should be here: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5094/changes#diff-221ca7d5a71aa06ecab51f0872030eb8ba2217afd79b1dd24f4987e0282b9004R107
Should the builder wait for the PTC votes before choosing the parent block hash to build their block on top of?

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
or can we have something like this:
```
// Takes in the potential candidate block to extend and the head beacon state
def should_extend_payload(store: Store, state: BeaconState, block: root) -> bool:
   # Payload for root has seen
   assert root in state.payloads
   # Blob data is available for block with `root`
   assert is_data_available(root)
   # root is the latest block header
   assert root == hash_tree_root(state.latest_block_header)
```
the above checks just asserts for local data availability, payload availability and whether the block has been successfully processed. But we dont check for PTC votes here, so the builder can create the block when it receives a valid execution payload envelope?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: should `should_extend_payload` block if it is called before the PTC BPS deadline and wait for the PTC votes?

author: nflaig
content: <@755590043632140352> this seems underspecified in the current gloas spec, there is no guidance at all when to build the payload or whether to extend or not? I am but confused because on `master` branch the `prepare_execution_payload` just takes in a state, but there is no notion or guidance or when or how to use post block state or post envelope state? maybe I am missing some how that works in current gloas spec

author: potuz
content: In the current Gloas spec this is not changed from phase 0, proposers call get_head and advance it to their current slot

author: potuz
content: Get head tells them to build on top of full or empty

author: nflaig
content: ok so in current gloas spec, `prepare_execution_payload` just assumes it's called with whatever head state the node sees from FC view?

author: potuz
content: Our current forkchoice is such that if block A is head and the Payload for that block arrives, then the Payload becomes head. But I think it's fragile to assume this will be the case forever and there's reasons to keep the door open for it not to be the case (like honest reorgs features today) so I think the right thing to do it to call get_head to find out on top of what you're building

author: potuz
content: Yes, correct, we always propose on top of head

author: nflaig
content: thanks <@755590043632140352> I am working on updating `prepare_execution_payload` to address Michael's concern, I think I know what to do

author: potuz
content: I am not worried about the spec itself but rather how to implement it, also probably the beacon API will need to provide some new endpoint for builders to make this partial state transition cause otherwise we will rely on them maintaining this bug prone path

author: nflaig
content: we can probably have something like https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/584, but I think this should be much simpler now, but Bharath can probably chime in here, I am getting the spec into a place now first so we see what's actually required in the prepare step

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah for proposing we might require such an API now but I would like to be clear on how `prepare_execution_payload` will look like. Do proposers need to maintain an intermediate state given by `process_parent_execution_payload` ?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: and that intermediate state is used in `prepare_execution_payload`

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I dont think we need an API here. We should do the partial state transition to compute the resulting payload attributes to send to the FCU. 
Then we just shoot these attributes via the SSE event to the builder

author: bharath.vedartham
content: just because a proposer sees a payload, i dont think it is true that the entire n/w will necessarily see the payload right? i think ideally waiting for the PTC votes would indicate that most of the n/w has seen the payload and extending on the payload is safe

author: bharath.vedartham
content: otherwise a proposer would risk extending on a payload that most of the n/w hasnt seen right?

author: potuz
content: No, local availability makes the Payload automatically head if the block is head. We do not rely on PTC for this. `should_extend_payload` is automatically true

author: bharath.vedartham
content: fair i was wondering if a proposer builds on a payload but that payload has not been seen by majority of the n/w, then that payload would get orphaned

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i think it would be difficult to assert a global availability on the payload eitherways. The PTC votes seem like a good indicator

author: potuz
content: This is the "honest reorg" feature that we may want to implement in the future but we decided that it's dangerous to do this for now. It opens a whole new discussion. Anyway it can't be avoided, if clients get forked, proposers can start reorging payloads as soon as they get a majority PTC vote for "unavailable". But this is not specified
